title: Paranal Observatory

infobox.value: ESO's Paranal Observatory [Edit this at Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q10914152 "Edit this at Wikidata")
[309](/wiki/List_of_observatory_codes#309 "List of observatory codes") [Edit this on Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q10914152?uselang=en#P717 "Edit this on Wikidata")
1 April 1999 [Edit this on Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q10914152?uselang=en#P1619 "Edit this on Wikidata")

is an [astronomical observatory](/wiki/Astronomical_observatory "Astronomical observatory") operated by the [European Southern Observatory](/wiki/European_Southern_Observatory "European Southern Observatory") (ESO). It is located in the [Atacama Desert](/wiki/Atacama_Desert "Atacama Desert") of Northern [Chile](/wiki/Chile "Chile") on [Cerro Paranal](/wiki/Cerro_Paranal "Cerro Paranal") at 2,635 m (8,645 ft) altitude, 120 km (70 mi) south of [Antofagasta](/wiki/Antofagasta "Antofagasta"). By total light-collecting area, it is the largest optical-infrared observatory in the Southern Hemisphere; worldwide, it is second to the [Mauna Kea Observatory](/wiki/Mauna_Kea_Observatory "Mauna Kea Observatory") on Hawaii.

**Paranal Observatory** is an [astronomical observatory](/wiki/Astronomical_observatory "Astronomical observatory") operated by the [European Southern Observatory](/wiki/European_Southern_Observatory "European Southern Observatory") (ESO). It is located in the [Atacama Desert](/wiki/Atacama_Desert "Atacama Desert") of Northern [Chile](/wiki/Chile "Chile") on [Cerro Paranal](/wiki/Cerro_Paranal "Cerro Paranal") at 2,635 m (8,645 ft) altitude, 120 km (70 mi) south of [Antofagasta](/wiki/Antofagasta "Antofagasta"). By total light-collecting area, it is the largest optical-infrared observatory in the Southern Hemisphere; worldwide, it is second to the [Mauna Kea Observatory](/wiki/Mauna_Kea_Observatory "Mauna Kea Observatory") on Hawaii.

The [Very Large Telescope](/wiki/Very_Large_Telescope "Very Large Telescope") (VLT), the largest telescope on Paranal, is composed of four separate 8.2 m (320 in) telescopes. In addition, the four main telescopes can be used simultaneously for extra light gathering capacity, and for [interferometry](/wiki/Interferometry "Interferometry"). Four auxiliary telescopes of 1.8 m (71 in) each are also part of the VLTI to make it available when the main telescopes are being used for other projects. The site also houses two survey telescopes with wide fields of view, the 4.0 m (160 in) [VISTA](/wiki/VISTA_(telescope) "VISTA (telescope)") and the 2.6 m (100 in) [VLT Survey Telescope](/wiki/VLT_Survey_Telescope "VLT Survey Telescope") for surveying large areas of the sky; and two arrays of small telescopes called [NGTS](/wiki/NGTS "NGTS") and [SPECULOOS](/wiki/SPECULOOS "SPECULOOS") which are dedicated to searching for [exoplanets](/wiki/Exoplanets "Exoplanets").

Two major new facilities are under construction nearby: the southern part of the [Cherenkov Telescope Array](/wiki/Cherenkov_Telescope_Array "Cherenkov Telescope Array") gamma-ray telescope (not owned by ESO) will be sited in the grounds 10 km south-east of Paranal; while ESO's future [E-ELT](/wiki/E-ELT "E-ELT") will be on the nearby peak of [Cerro Armazones](/wiki/Cerro_Armazones "Cerro Armazones") 20 km east of Paranal, and will share some of the base facilities.

## Telescopes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paranal_Observatory&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Telescopes")]

### Aerial view

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paranal_Observatory&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Aerial view")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6b/View_of_the_Very_Large_Telescope.jpg/330px-View_of_the_Very_Large_Telescope.jpg)](/wiki/File:View_of_the_Very_Large_Telescope.jpg)

The four [VLTs](/wiki/Very_Large_Telescope "Very Large Telescope") and ATs and the [VST](/wiki/VLT_Survey_Telescope "VLT Survey Telescope") on the main peak and [VISTA](/wiki/VISTA_(telescope) "VISTA (telescope)") in the background

From an aerial view of the Paranal Observatory, the four large units of the [VLT](/wiki/Very_Large_Telescope "Very Large Telescope") with their four small, dome-shaped auxiliary telescopes can be clearly seen. The Survey Telescope, [VST](/wiki/VLT_Survey_Telescope "VLT Survey Telescope"), is immediately adjacent to the VLT and seen in between two of its units, while [VISTA](/wiki/VISTA_(telescope) "VISTA (telescope)") is located on a secondary peak, some 1,500 m away in the background *(see image)*.

### Very Large Telescope

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paranal_Observatory&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Very Large Telescope")]

Main article: [Very Large Telescope](/wiki/Very_Large_Telescope "Very Large Telescope")

The [Very Large Telescope](/wiki/Very_Large_Telescope "Very Large Telescope") (VLT) consists of four 8.2-metre telescopes operating in the [visible](/wiki/Visible_light "Visible light") and [infrared](/wiki/Infrared "Infrared"). These telescopes, along with four smaller auxiliary telescopes, are also combined to operate as an [optical interferometer](/wiki/Astronomical_optical_interferometry "Astronomical optical interferometry") on certain nights of the year. All of the 8.2-metre telescopes have [adaptive optics](/wiki/Adaptive_optics "Adaptive optics") and a full suite of instruments. The primary mirrors of the main telescopes are 8.2 meters in diameter but, in practice, the pupil of the telescopes is defined by the secondary mirrors, effectively limiting the usable diameter to 8.0 meters.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

### VISTA Survey Telescope

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paranal_Observatory&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: VISTA Survey Telescope")]

Main article: [VISTA (telescope)](/wiki/VISTA_(telescope) "VISTA (telescope)")

[VISTA](/wiki/VISTA_(telescope) "VISTA (telescope)") is the *Visible & Infrared Survey Telescope for Astronomy*, a 4.0-metre telescope with a wide [field of view](/wiki/Field_of_view "Field of view"), focusing on infrared [surveys](/wiki/Astronomical_survey "Astronomical survey") of the sky. It was built close to [ESO's](/wiki/European_Southern_Observatory "European Southern Observatory") [VLT](/wiki/Very_Large_Telescope "Very Large Telescope") by a consortium of 18 universities from the United Kingdom, led by [Queen Mary University of London](/wiki/Queen_Mary_University_of_London "Queen Mary University of London"). VISTA was handed over to the European Southern Observatory in December 2009.

### VLT Survey Telescope (VST)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paranal_Observatory&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: VLT Survey Telescope (VST)")]

Main article: [VLT Survey Telescope](/wiki/VLT_Survey_Telescope "VLT Survey Telescope")

The [VLT Survey Telescope](/wiki/VLT_Survey_Telescope "VLT Survey Telescope") or VST is a 2.6-metre telescope with a wide field imager intended to aid the four Very Large Telescope (VLTs) in their scientific aims.

### Next-Generation Transit Survey (NGTS)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paranal_Observatory&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Next-Generation Transit Survey (NGTS)")]

Main article: [Next-Generation Transit Survey](/wiki/Next-Generation_Transit_Survey "Next-Generation Transit Survey")

The [Next-Generation Transit Survey](/wiki/Next-Generation_Transit_Survey "Next-Generation Transit Survey") (NGTS) is an [exoplanet](/wiki/Exoplanet "Exoplanet")-survey facility located a few kilometers from the main peak. It consists of an array of twelve 0.2-meter robotic telescopes with a very large field of view of 96 square degrees or several hundred times the area of the full moon. The survey aims to discover numerous [super-Earths](/wiki/Super-Earth "Super-Earth") and Neptune-sized planets around nearby stars, using [transit photometry](/wiki/Transit_method "Transit method") to detect them. NGTS is managed by a partnership of seven academic institutions from Chile, Germany, Switzerland and the United Kingdom and its design is based on the [SuperWASP](/wiki/SuperWASP "SuperWASP") project. Science operations began in early 2015.[[2]](#cite_note-eso1502-2)[[3]](#cite_note-ngts-3)

### SPECULOOS Southern Observatory (SSO)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paranal_Observatory&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: SPECULOOS Southern Observatory (SSO)")]

Main article: [SPECULOOS](/wiki/SPECULOOS "SPECULOOS")

The SPECULOOS Southern Observatory (SSO) consists out of four telescopes, called Europa, Io, Callisto, and Ganymede. ESO hosts the 1-meter telescopes and the project is led by the [University of Liège](/wiki/University_of_Li%C3%A8ge "University of Liège"). The project aims to find [exoplanets](/wiki/Exoplanet "Exoplanet") around [ultracool dwarf](/wiki/Ultra-cool_dwarf "Ultra-cool dwarf") stars and [brown dwarfs](/wiki/Brown_dwarf "Brown dwarf"). The project builds on the success of the [TRAPPIST](/wiki/TRAPPIST "TRAPPIST") telescope. The telescopes are located next to the NGTS.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Other buildings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paranal_Observatory&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Other buildings")]

Further information: [ESO Hotel](/wiki/ESO_Hotel "ESO Hotel")

As well as the telescopes, control buildings and maintenance facilities, Paranal has a [Residencia](/wiki/ESO_Residencia "ESO Residencia") which provides accommodation for staff and visitors. This is located 200 m lower and 3 km from the telescopes. It is built half into the mountain with the concrete coloured to blend into the landscape. It has gym facilities, a swimming pool, a restaurant and two gardens. The construction was decorated by the Chilean [architect](/wiki/Architect "Architect") Paula Gutiérrez Erlandsen, [Marchioness of la Pica](/wiki/Marquis_of_la_Pica "Marquis of la Pica").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Popular culture and other uses

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paranal_Observatory&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Popular culture and other uses")]

To illustrate the isolation of the Paranal Observatory from the lights of civilization, it is located 38 kilometres (24 mi) in straight-line distance north of [Paposo](/wiki/Paposo "Paposo"), population 259, the nearest community to the observatory.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

The VLT hotel, the Residencia, served as a backdrop for part of the 2008 James Bond film *[Quantum of Solace](/wiki/Quantum_of_Solace "Quantum of Solace")*.

The observatory's facilities were used to stage the [Pacific Alliance](/wiki/Pacific_Alliance "Pacific Alliance")'s fourth summit in June 2012, formally launching the organization.

On 14 March 2013, [Frederik, Crown Prince of Denmark](/wiki/Frederik_X "Frederik X"), accompanied by his wife, [Princess Mary](/wiki/Mary,_Crown_Princess_of_Denmark "Mary, Crown Princess of Denmark"), visited ESO's Paranal Observatory, as part of an official visit to Chile. Their tour of Paranal's astronomical facilities was led by ESO's Director General, Tim de Zeeuw.

The visit of the [Prince](/wiki/Felipe,_Prince_of_Asturias "Felipe, Prince of Asturias") and [Princess of Asturias](/wiki/Letizia,_Princess_of_Asturias "Letizia, Princess of Asturias") to the observatory

[The Crown Prince of Denmark](/wiki/Frederik_X "Frederik X") visits the observatory.
